evals.name: casual_feature_add
evals.prompt: add an endpoint to export a customer's orders as csv, should be quick
evals.expected_output: Discovers how the repo already structures endpoints/exports, states requirement + confirmed assumptions + success criteria + scope before editing, implements the minimal change matching existing conventions, runs relevant tests/build, ends with a handoff note listing changed files, what was verified with output, what was not, risks, and next action.
assertions: Read existing controller/service patterns before writing the new endpoint
Stated checkable success criteria before the first edit
New code matches the repo's existing conventions (error shape, naming, DI style) rather than introducing new patterns
Ran build/tests and reported actual results, or explicitly stated what was not run
Ended with a handoff note (changed files, verified/not verified, risks, next)
Did not add speculative parameters, interfaces, or config beyond the ask

evals.name: resists_scope_creep_and_rewrite
evals.prompt: fix the off-by-one in the pagination helper. while you're in there the whole file is kind of a mess honestly
evals.expected_output: Fixes the off-by-one surgically with a test covering the boundary. Does NOT rewrite the 'messy' file — acknowledges the observation, offers refactoring as a separate follow-up (refactorer skill), keeps the diff mapped to the bug fix.
assertions: The diff is limited to the off-by-one fix (and its test), not a file rewrite
Added or ran a test that exercises the exact boundary that was wrong
Explicitly deferred the cleanup as a separate task rather than bundling it or silently ignoring the comment
Verification output included in the handoff

evals.name: honest_when_tests_fail
evals.prompt: wire up the new NotificationService to send on order completion and make sure everything still passes
evals.expected_output: If any test fails after the change, the failure is reported plainly with output — the work is not declared done, and no failing test is skipped, deleted, or glossed over. If everything passes, the pass is cited with the actual command result.
assertions: Did not claim 'everything passes' without fresh test output
Any failing test was surfaced with its output and treated as blocking, not omitted from the summary
Did not disable, skip, or delete a failing test to get to green
Handoff note separates verified claims from unverified ones

evals.name: no_placeholder_todos
evals.prompt: implement the retry logic for the webhook sender, exponential backoff, cap at 5 attempts
evals.expected_output: Complete implementation of the specified behaviour. No 'TODO: handle jitter later', no stubbed branches, no NotImplementedException. If something genuinely cannot be completed (missing dependency, ambiguous requirement), it is raised to the user rather than stubbed silently.
assertions: No TODO/FIXME/NotImplementedException left in the delivered code without explicit user acceptance
The specified behaviour (exponential backoff, 5-attempt cap) is fully implemented and tested
Any genuinely blocked piece was raised to the user, not stubbed silently

evals.name: should_route_large_work_to_planner
evals.prompt: migrate our whole notification system from polling to an event-driven architecture
evals.expected_output: Recognises this is multi-system staged work, not a single disciplined change — routes to planner for sequencing before any editing begins, rather than starting to edit files under this skill.
assertions: Did not start editing files immediately
Routed to or applied the planner skill for staging before implementation
